The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

Despite Salieri's Machiavellian attempts to frustrate Mozart's progress, the great young composer attracts the favour of the Emperor.

programme.desc: Tenacious meerkat pups show their determination to devour deadly scorpions in the vast Kalahari Desert, while a group of lionesses' demonstrate their seduction tactics in Kenya.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Go rogue with frigate birds who steal meals from smaller seabirds, relax in a mongoose-run spa with a grateful warthog, decode an African elephant's secret message, and watch lovelorn capuchin monkeys hurl rocks to attract prospective mates.

programme.title: Wild Tales From The Farm
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and life on the farm is bursting out everywhere, as twin lambs Larry and Flora leap for joy in the meadow with the other newborns.

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: Discover how weirdness is baked into some animals' DNA from birth. Check out the bowerbird's mate-attracting dance moves, the Christmas Island crab's traffic-dodging prowess, and the sockeye salmon's uphill swimming skills.

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: A look at the abilities that give some animals an edge in life, including fireflies' flashes, the tarsier's night vision, the mantis shrimp's knockout punch, and the saltwater croc's prey-detecting pressure sensors.

programme.title: Wild Survivors
programme.desc: The drive to reproduce is the one of the most primal forces in nature. It dictates how any given species will behave for most of its life. There are over a hundred thousand species of animal in Africa, so there are countless strategies to find a mate.

programme.title: Wild Survivors
programme.desc: Finding food is the most immediate need for any animal. Food is the fuel that gives an animal strength to fight, or to flee. But in the Luangwa valley the dry season is at its height and the landscape has become cruel and barren.

programme.title: Wild Survivors
programme.desc: Zambia's Luangwa Valley is a vast wilderness. It may seem ageless and permanent but in reality it's in constant flux, influenced by the myriad of animals that call it home. Animals don't just live in a given environment, they help to shape it.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Koala
programme.desc: Koalas live in isolated forests in east and southeast Australia.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Koala
programme.desc: The behaviour of the koala changes dramatically during mating season.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David descends beneath the waves at night in the state of the art Triton submersible, the first of its kind to visit the reef. He meets some of the tiny coral animals that built the reef and helped to turn it into an underwater wonderland.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David continues his exploration of the Great Barrier Reef, aboard the research vessel the Alucia. He discovers the incredible animals that visit the reef every year, from birds to whales, some travelling thousands of kilometres to get there.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David examines what the next few decades hold for this remarkable community of animals, as well as what is being done to save it.

programme.title: Deadly Australians

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Young gladiators earn their stripes in the dense tropical forests of the Americas. Watch as a poison dart frog unleashes a venomous barrage, observe a turf war between a jaguar and a caiman, and watch a bear cross paths with a defending tarantula.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Head to America's far north for a look at the ultimate brawlers living along the waterways. See how spring thaw fuels rivalries and eagles take on herons, bears against bears and dragonfly nymphs against venomous giant bugs.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: When Lovejoy gets lumbered with a giant sideboard, he blames Eric as usual, but Eric is holidaying with Tinker at his Uncle Jack's pub on the Essex marshes, and finds himself involved in a smuggling racket.

programme.title: World at War

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: America entered conflict after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, but the conflict in Europe and the conflict in the Pacific were two separate wars. Many Americans would have been content to forget about Hitler and Europe. Despite opposition from congress President Roosevelt, however, was committed to fighting Hitler. Eventually Hitler declared war on America, thus relieving Roosevelt of a difficult decision.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explore Oradour-sur-Glane, a French town drenched in infamy, the victim of a notorious raid, Alcatraz, a facility famed for breakouts, subjected to a daring break-in, and Sealand, a micronation off the English coast, stormed by a band of mercenaries.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The Tunnels of Gibraltar were a Second World War espionage base, hidden in the Rock of Gibraltar, which lay undiscovered for decades. At Atlas-1 Test Site, are the remains of an ingenious US Airforce project that continues to keep people safe.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In the Scottish capital of Edinburgh, hidden from view, a series of vaults tell a murky story of opportunity, crime and destitution. At Fort Schwatka Unalaska are the remains of an Alaskan battleground, where a foreign invader set foot on American soil for the first time since 1812.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

At Fort Schwatka Unalaska are the remains of an Alaskan battleground, where a foreign invader set foot on American soil for the first time since 1812.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

programme.title: The Two Ronnies

programme.desc: Mr Newbold has decided to barricade his house against Mrs Featherstone, but Granville is sure he wouldn't be so keen to avoid her if he knew all about her hidden exotic past. programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. That's where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A puppy called Albie has a broken back from being knocked over by a car and needs a complex operation. Also, a Scottish terrier has a mysterious ailment never seen before and Noel must use old-fashioned detective work to find out what is wrong.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel faces one of his greatest ever practical and ethical challenges as he seeks to provide Hermes, the one-legged tortoise, with three new prosthetic feet. Also, a Rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Keira, a dog on the brink of starvation, and gentle old Jack Russell Paige, whose owner had to make the heartbreaking decision to give her up after 15 years.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps rehabilitate a group of ex-racing greyhounds who have never experienced life beyond the racetrack.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, who's been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike, a cash-strapped young couple struggling to buy their first home, think their prayers are answered when a distant relative bequeaths them a sprawling estate. However, the old house is haunted by the ghosts of its former inhabitants.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Still recovering from her near-fatal accident, Alison finds herself hounded by the ghostly gaggle, who now realise that she can both see and hear them. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the old house into a home leads to a DIY disaster.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: On Pat's `death day', he prepares himself for his family's annual pilgrimage to the site of his demise. Realising that Alison's gift makes her a conduit between the dead and the living, he begs her to pass on a message from the other side.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: The Smith family embark upon a mammoth declutter. Stacey and the team challenge them to let go of half of their possessions, so that they can beautifully reorganise their life and home. programme.desc: Still recovering from her near-fatal accident, Alison finds herself hounded by the ghostly gaggle, who now realise that she can both see and hear them. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the old house into a home leads to a DIY disaster.

programme.desc: Officers from Sun Hill are called to deal with an apparently motiveless attack in a pub.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil has to deal with some demons from the past when a woman is assaulted.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil is convinced that someone has a vendetta against Julia Bickham. Jared Miles proves to be innocent and identifies a man he saw with Thomas Sands - Stephen Fairfax. It emerges that Julia gave evidence at the inquest a couple of years before of a young woman suicide which meant that Fairfax lost an insurance claim and the custody of his daughter. Then, he kidnaps Julia.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When the police receive an emergency call informing them that a newsagent called O'Halloran has been beaten to death, Banksy is shocked to learn that the anonymous message came from Joseph Sissoulu's mobile. Prints found at the scene are traced to Mo Campbell - a friend of Joseph's murdered son. Mo eventually admits to the murder, but is he telling the whole story?

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DI Neil Manson and DS Max Carter investigate when 17-year-old Ashley Simmons is found shot dead and dumped in a warehouse. Their enquiries lead them to the children's home where she once lived, but friends and family there all claim that she was a clean-living teen. Evidence points to Ashley's mother's boyfriend, whose car was spotted at the crime scene.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DS Max Carter and DC Jo Masters question Paul Brewer when a raid discloses indecent images of a young girl on his laptop. While at first denying responsibilty, Paul then points the finger of suspicion at paedophile George Fielding, a man he met in prison, and to whom he had recently lent the computer. When DC Jacob Banks questions Fielding, he immediately verifies Paul's story, but Banks is suspicious of both men's statements.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max and Jo raid the house of Jimmy Mack but fail to find his consignment of drugs - or his missing girlfriend, Rochelle. Sally and Roger visit the home of former prostitute and drug addict Fran Morris, where her young son Noah has collapsed. Then Rochelle is found tied to a chair in Fran's flat, having been beaten up.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for the people inside.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A man is left with a metal spike impaled in his face after a traumatic incident on a construction site, sparking a huge rescue operation. Plus, medics help a man who was hit by a car.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman plunges 10ft through the floorboards of her Sheffield home in a freak accident, and the HART team launch a major rescue operation to save her.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: There's a major rescue operation on the M1 after a car towing a caravan rolls over and hits a lamp post - trapping the driver. A teacher out on his mountain bike suffers a brain injury.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team helps a biker critically injured in a crash. In Brontë country, a man vanishes after going for a swim in a Pennines lake, sparking a major rescue operation.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A teenage forestry worker receives a life-threatening injury; severe asthma endangers a mum's life; the Police help young divers safely to shore.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A woman is caught on the wrong end of a fishing spear; a daring rescue at sea; a chopper crew welcomes a new life mid-flight; the Police catch a boatie making waves.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When the local council insists that Ben is disabled, what can he do but play along with it until the wheels start coming off?

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The Victorian London sewer system is getting a 21st century upgrade. Machines bore a network of tunnels linking Bazalgette's old sewer network to the Tideway Super-Sewer.

programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and collection of tractors, but extracting them from a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated.

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitler's Island Slaves
programme.desc: Documentary on the artist Piers Secunda's research into the use of slave labour on the British Channel island during World War II.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Dulwich Art Gallery in South London. Among the objects brought in by the public to excite the experts are an early and little-known photograph of Winston Churchill, a theatrical costume dating from the early days of pantomime, and one of the most valuable paintings seen on the show in recent years.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Dulwich Picture Gallery in South London to uncover more intriguing heirlooms, including an early illustration by madcap artist Heath Robinson.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales in order to purchase a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree, with their own names: Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitler's Island Slaves
programme.desc: Documentary on the artist Piers Secunda's research into the use of slave labour on the British Channel island during World War II.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: We witness some of the most intimate and surprising behaviours of marine mammals. Raising the bar on our understanding of how whales and dolphins make their lives at sea, as well as meeting those terrestrial mammals that dip their toes, and sometimes more, into the watery world.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: Having honed their survival strategies over millennia, mammals have evolved to be masters of the cold. From icy seas to snow-capped mountains, a look at the unique mammals that call these frozen worlds home.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The festive season falls upon the inmates of HMP Slade and finds them planning an escape. Fletch, meanwhile, is certain that the plan will fail and that retribution for it will be extremely unpleasant, so he plans to spend some time in the infirmary. The doctor, however, likes to keep the place empty.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher plans to add a bit of Christmas spirit to the festive season at Slade Prison in the form of his very own home-made liquor, but prison officers Mackay and Barraclough uncover his stash.

programme.title: Attenborough's Birds of Paradise
programme.desc: David Attenborough highlights the mystery and scientific revelation surrounding the elusive birds.

programme.title: Attenborough's Wonder of Eggs
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores his passion for birds' eggs. These remarkable structures nurture new life, protecting it from the outside world at the same time as allowing it to breathe. They are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. How is an egg made? Why are they the shape they are?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: It's a first for the boys as they set their sights on a Saab. But when all hopes of a classic 99 are dashed, they're forced to settle for a failed rally car.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions. Henry and Simon realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast iron fire guard and old wooden workshop clamps.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: 300,000 sq miles of ocean. Three GPS-tracked voyages, filmed around the clock. One week at sea. Emilia Jayne hunts for cuttlefish in the world's busiest shipping lane. Cargo ships have right of way here, but lucrative cuttlefish swim in these grounds too. After finding good fishing at the start of his week-long trip, can skipper Luke Selvey keep the secret to himself? Margaret of Ladram is Brixham's most successful trawler.

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along The US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border, in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she tries just about every sample in a Tequila showroom and is left in no doubt that the city deserves its reputation as a party town. She also discovers an unexpected invasion of migrants: American retirees who have settled south of the border to enjoy the good life and an ocean view at an affordable price. Over on the US side of the border, Sue spends a day with Mark Lamb, sheriff of Pinal County.

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along The US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Sue visits the Mexican city of Juarez, essentially one half of a super-city adjoining El Paso, Texas. Here she meets people whose lives traverse the border, including Gabriela, who is bringing up her kids alone in El Paso because her husband, Adrian, is not legal in America and is stuck in Juarez. Every weekend she takes the children to the border, so they can cross into Mexico and be with their dad. But Gabriela is not allowed to cross the border until her own immigration papers are finalised.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will and Gus buy a Porsche 923 with a cracked interior, bad paintwork and ripped seats. This driving legend is well past its best, but can they still turn a profit?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will and Gus look to make some quick cash as they fix and wrap a Toyota MR2 MK2. But is there money to be made in this low-spec car?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will benefit from an owner's decision that his broken vehicle is beyond repair, and secure a Mercedes 190 investment that they hope to restore.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve visits the tropical Indian Ocean islands of Madagascar, Mauritius and the Seychelles. programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: By pushing themselves and their bodies to the limit, mammals have found remarkable ways to survive in the hottest places on earth. In baking deserts, harsh dry spiny forests, barren desolate salt pans and even bush fires, mammals beat the heat.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough reveals why forests are the ultimate test of survival for mammals and how they have overcome the challenges of life in this multidimensional, complex and continually changing environment.

programme.desc: Derek travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave is on HQ duties and admires a Punto that's two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock.
